title: Meta will use public Instagram photos to train its AI models: how to avoid it
summary: Users can disable the use of their images in the social network's privacy settings.

title: AMD Zen6 Medusa Point 10-core APU outperforms Ryzen AI 9 HX 370
summary: Benchmark leaks show a 22% improvement in Geekbench compared to current processors.

title: China boosts its chip industry amid uncertainty over US sanctions
summary: Major Chinese tech companies bet on local manufacturers, while a chip alliance with Russia is being forged.

title: The New York Times accuses OpenAI of hiding evidence in copyright trial
summary: OpenAI allegedly deleted ChatGPT records and concealed the inability to search training data.

title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.6 and ChatGPT Work, an agent to automate workflows
summary: The new model matches Fable 5 at a third of the cost and is deployed alongside an automation tool.

title: xAI introduces Grok 4.5 with faster coding and lower prices
summary: Grok 4.5 offers competitive performance at a much lower cost than GPT-5.5 and Fable 5.

title: Chinese open-source model GLM 5.2 matches human accuracy in accounting
summary: Databricks adopts GLM 5.2 as its default coding engine after demonstrating efficiency and low cost.

title: OpenAI launches GPT-Live, voice models that allow listening and speaking at the same time
summary: The new voice models offer real-time interaction, speaking and listening simultaneously.

title: The DuckDuckGo browser automatically blocks YouTube ads on all platforms
summary: The feature is available without extensions and for free on both desktop and mobile.

title: AMD revives the Zen2 Ryzen 7 4700LE processor in a low-cost PC with RTX 3050
summary: The old 8-core processor returns in a pre-assembled $800 PC with discrete graphics.

title: Minisforum launches a mini PC with 128 GB RAM and support for low-profile GPU
summary: The new Panther Lake workstation is designed for intensive tasks and supports discrete graphics.

title: The European Parliament reactivates the Chat Control 1.0 law for mass scanning of communications
summary: Through a parliamentary procedure, message control was approved despite previous opposition.

title: Ivy League professor uncovers AI cheating by switching to in-person exam, grades drop 50%
summary: Only two students maintained their grade when the exam was held in person versus the virtual format.

title: Google Photos incorporates Video Remix, a fast AI video editing tool
summary: The new feature allows creating creative clips quickly using generative AI.

title: HalluSquatting: new attack exploits AI hallucinations to execute malicious code
summary: AI-based security systems can be deceived by exploiting their generative errors.

title: Intel prepares 28-core Nova Lake-S processors for workstations
summary: Leaked details reveal specifications of upcoming high-performance chips with Xe3 graphics.

title: Meta launches Muse Spark 1.1 API with prices that squeeze OpenAI and Anthropic
summary: The new prices reduce the cost of accessing AI models, intensifying the price war.

title: New York bans smart glasses in courts, while Pamplona uses them in San Fermín
summary: Two recent incidents highlight the debate around the use of smart glasses in public spaces.

title: PlayStation pushes for the end of physical format, but Halloween rescues discs with physical editions
summary: The video game industry experiences tension between the abandonment of physical media and the release of new disc editions.

title: Advances in robotic hands: Chinese and American companies compete for artificial dexterity
summary: 1X's Neo robot and Chinese innovations show significant progress in robotic manipulation.
